refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with the high safety criticality by ensuring operators have visibility into energy consumption patterns.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_tonnage_moved) across a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work week). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site energy meters. Confirm operator feedback mechanisms are functional and used.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall transmit energy and productivity telemetry to the operator (local or remote) in real-time via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide operator-accessible feedback (dashboard, alerts, recommendations) on energy efficiency status relative to E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall support tele-operator control modes that enable remote energy-efficient operation without compromising safety.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency KPI data for post-evaluation analysis and continuous improvement.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy metering at the Vehicle power subsystem; transmit raw energy and tonnage data via Telemetry to a centralized analytics engine; provide operator dashboard with real-time KPI display and efficiency alerts.

DP-B: Embed energy optimization logic within Vehicle control firmware; use local feedback loops to minimize energy per work cycle; expose efficiency metrics via Telemetry; operator monitors via dashboard.

DP-C: Integrate energy monitoring across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; use multi-modal feedback (visual, auditory, haptic) to guide operator behavior; implement predictive energy models to recommend efficient operation modes.
DP-D: Implement passive energy logging at Vehicle level only; provide batch reporting post-shift; no real-time feedback or optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Integrate energy monitoring across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; use multi-modal feedback (visual, auditory, haptic) to guide operator behavior; implement predictive energy models to recommend efficient operation modes.

potential_sources: Energy monitoring logic (Vehicle) is decoupled from operator feedback rendering (Teleoperation) via Telemetry interface.
Safety Services integration ensures energy optimization does not compromise safety constraints (e.g., no speed reduction below safe limits).
Predictive models operate independently of real-time control, reducing coupling between optimization and immediate vehicle response.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits loose coupling across modules via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). The multi-modal feedback approach is modular: visual feedback (dashboard), auditory (alerts), and haptic (if supported by Teleoperation) can be implemented independently. Predictive models can be updated offline without affecting real-time control.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle power subsystem can reliably measure energy consumption with ±5% accuracy.
ASSUMPTION: Telemetry interface has sufficient bandwidth and latency (<500ms) to support real-time energy feedback.
ASSUMPTION: Operators will respond to efficiency feedback and modify behavior accordingly.
RISK: Predictive energy models may be inaccurate in novel worksite conditions, leading to misleading recommendations.
RISK: Multi-modal feedback could overwhelm operators if not carefully designed; may reduce safety focus.
RISK: Integration across three modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) increases system complexity and testing burden.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) energy metering location and accuracy requirements, (2) Telemetry payload schema for energy data, (3) feedback design principles (e.g., alert thresholds, modality selection rules), (4) safety constraints that bound energy optimization (e.g., minimum speed, maximum load), (5) predictive model update frequency and validation criteria.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that orchestrates energy efficiency monitoring and feedback across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It owns the integration logic, data flow coordination, and KPI computation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional: Vehicle transmits energy consumption (kWh), tonnage moved, and operational state; NGHE Solution aggregates and computes energy_per_ton KPI; transmits efficiency feedback and recommendations back to Teleoperation for operator display.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives work tasks (e.g., move X tons of material) from Site Manager or Tele-operator; uses task context to set energy efficiency targets and validate KPI achievement.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints from Safety Services (e.g., speed limits, load limits); ensures energy optimization recommendations do not violate safety bounds; transmits energy-related safety alerts (e.g., overheating due to inefficient operation) to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to coordinate data exchange between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; ensures reliable delivery of energy telemetry and feedback in real-time or near-real-time.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy efficiency KPI logic. It integrates Vehicle metering, Teleoperation feedback, and Safety Services constraints. Minimal diffusion: only the Telemetry interface boundary is shared with Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Vehicle owns energy metering hardware and local power management. However, KPI computation (energy_per_ton), feedback rendering, and safety constraint integration belong at NGHE Solution level. If energy efficiency logic were entirely in Vehicle, it would duplicate across multiple vehicle types and complicate tele-operator feedback.

Teleoperation owns operator feedback rendering (dashboard, alerts, haptic). However, energy data aggregation, KPI computation, and predictive models should not be embedded in Teleoperation; they are system-level concerns. High diffusion risk if energy logic is scattered across local and remote operator interfaces.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement and alert generation. Energy efficiency optimization must respect safety bounds, but the optimization logic itself is not a safety function. Assigning energy efficiency to Safety Services would conflate two distinct concerns and increase coupling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the optimal owner of CR-NGHE-003 because: (1) it sits at the system integration level, coordinating data from Vehicle (metering), Teleoperation (operator feedback), and Safety Services (constraints); (2) it avoids diffusion of energy efficiency logic across multiple modules; (3) it enables reuse of energy optimization algorithms across different vehicle types and worksites; (4) it provides a clear interface contract (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) for all stakeholders.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module,' it may accumulate unrelated responsibilities over time. Mitigation: define clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Energy Monitoring, KPI Computation, Feedback Orchestration).

RISK: Real-time energy feedback may require low-latency processing; if NGHE Solution is deployed remotely (cloud), latency could exceed acceptable thresholds. Mitigation: consider edge deployment or hybrid architecture (local + cloud).

RISK: Predictive energy models may require frequent updates based on new worksite data; if model management is not clearly owned, updates may be delayed or inconsistent. Mitigation: assign explicit ownership of model lifecycle (training, validation, deployment) within NGHE Solution or delegate to a dedicated ML operations module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution IM scope to explicitly define: (1) sub-module structure (Energy Monitoring, KPI Computation, Feedback Orchestration, Predictive Models), (2) deployment topology (edge vs. cloud vs. hybrid), (3) data retention and logging policies for energy KPI audit, (4) interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (message schemas, latency SLAs, error handling).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (integrated energy monitoring with multi-modal feedback and predictive models) directly addresses the refined FR (enable energy_per_ton ≤ E_target through monitoring, control optimization, and feedback). The DP specifies mechanisms (metering, analytics, feedback, safety integration) that logically satisfy the FR. No independence violations detected; energy monitoring and feedback are tightly coupled by design.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: Energy metering logic is distributed across Vehicle (hardware) and NGHE Solution (aggregation/KPI). Mitigation: define clear interface contract (Telemetry schema) to minimize coupling.
DIFFUSION RISK: Operator feedback rendering spans Teleoperation (display) and NGHE Solution (data preparation). Mitigation: NGHE Solution prepares feedback data; Teleoperation owns rendering logic.

DIFFUSION RISK: Safety constraint enforcement involves both Safety Services (constraint definition) and NGHE Solution (constraint application in optimization). Mitigation: Safety Services defines constraints via SafetyAlert interface; NGHE Solution applies them without modifying constraint logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the clear owner of energy efficiency KPI logic. Vehicle and Teleoperation are supporting modules with well-defined interfaces. Safety Services provides constraint boundaries. No module duplication or orphaned logic detected. Containment score of 95 for NGHE Solution is justified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) measure energy_per_ton via Vehicle metering and NGHE Solution KPI computation, (2) validate against E_target over agreed evaluation period, (3) audit operator feedback logs to confirm real-time visibility, (4) test safety constraint enforcement via SafetyAlert interface. All verification activities map to clear module responsibilities.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle owns metering hardware; NGHE Solution owns KPI computation and feedback orchestration; Teleoperation owns operator interface; Safety Services owns constraint definition. No ambiguity in responsibility boundaries.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify energy metering accuracy requirements (e.g., ±5%), Telemetry latency SLA (e.g., <500ms), and feedback design principles (e.g., alert thresholds, modality selection).
Define NGHE Solution sub-module structure (Energy Monitoring, KPI Computation, Feedback Orchestration, Predictive Models) to prevent monolithic growth.
Establish data retention and audit policies for energy KPI logs to support post-evaluation analysis and continuous improvement.
Specify deployment topology (edge vs. cloud vs. hybrid) to ensure real-time feedback latency requirements are met.
Define model lifecycle management for predictive energy models (training, validation, deployment, update frequency).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic; it comprises five sub-FRs (measurement, telemetry, feedback, tele-operation, and reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define concrete DPs and IMs. The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass all gates, but the system is complex enough to warrant hierarchical decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will clarify module responsibilities, interface contracts, and verification strategies.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall transmit energy and productivity telemetry to the operator (local or remote) in real-time via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide operator-accessible feedback (dashboard, alerts, recommendations) on energy efficiency status relative to E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall support tele-operator control modes that enable remote energy-efficient operation without compromising safety.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency KPI data for post-evaluation analysis and continuous improvement.

assumptions: Vehicle power subsystem can reliably measure energy consumption with ±5% accuracy.
Telemetry interface has sufficient bandwidth and latency (<500ms) to support real-time energy feedback.
Operators will respond to efficiency feedback and modify behavior accordingly.
E_target is defined by customer and remains stable over the evaluation period.
Worksite conditions (terrain, material type, weather) are representative of typical use cases.
Safety constraints (speed limits, load limits) are well-defined and enforced by Safety Services.
open_risks: Predictive energy models may be inaccurate in novel worksite conditions, leading to misleading recommendations.
Multi-modal feedback could overwhelm operators if not carefully designed; may reduce safety focus.
Integration across three modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) increases system complexity and testing burden.
Real-time energy feedback may require low-latency processing; if NGHE Solution is deployed remotely (cloud), latency could exceed acceptable thresholds.
Model management for predictive energy models may be unclear; updates could be delayed or inconsistent.
Energy efficiency optimization may conflict with other objectives (e.g., speed, comfort); trade-offs must be explicitly managed.
evidence_needed: Vehicle power metering specifications and calibration data.
Telemetry interface bandwidth and latency measurements under typical worksite conditions.
Operator feedback design guidelines and usability testing results.
Safety constraint definitions and enforcement mechanisms (from Safety Services).
Predictive energy model validation results on representative worksite data.
Post-evaluation KPI data from pilot deployments to confirm energy_per_ton ≤ E_target achievement.
Operator training materials and adoption metrics for energy efficiency feedback.
